audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Pentecost, Spirit/breath/fire/tongues, Peter's Joel/Psalm speech, turning/release, baptism, communal life.

verses.restored: When the day of Pentecost was being fulfilled, they were all together in the same place.
Suddenly there came from heaven a sound like a violent rushing breath/wind, and it filled the whole house where they were sitting.
There appeared to them divided tongues as of fire, and it sat upon each one of them.
They were all filled with holy Spirit and began to speak in other tongues, just as the Spirit was giving them to utter.
Now there were Judeans dwelling in Jerusalem, devout people from every nation under heaven.
When this sound happened, the multitude came together and was confused, because each one heard them speaking in one's own dialect.
They were amazed and marveling, saying, Look, are not all these speaking Galileans?
How is it that we each hear in our own native dialect?
Parthians, Medes, Elamites, those dwelling in Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya toward Cyrene, and visiting Romans,
both Judeans and proselytes, Cretans and Arabians, we hear them speaking in our tongues the great things of the Divine.
All were amazed and perplexed, saying to one another, What does this wish to be?
But others, mocking, were saying, They are filled with new wine.
Peter, standing with the eleven, lifted his voice and declared to them, Men of Judea and all dwelling in Jerusalem, let this be known to you and listen to my words.
For these are not drunk, as you suppose, for it is the third hour of the day.
But this is what has been spoken through the prophet Joel:

And it will be in the last days, says the Divine, I will pour out from my Spirit upon all flesh; your sons and daughters will prophesy, your young ones will see visions, and your elders will dream dreams.
Even upon my male servants and female servants in those days I will pour out from my Spirit, and they will prophesy.
I will give wonders in heaven above and signs on earth below, blood and fire and vapor of smoke.
The sun will be turned into darkness and the moon into blood before the great and manifest day of the Lord comes.
And it will be that everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord will be saved/healed.
Men of Israel, hear these words: Jesus the Nazarene, a man attested to you by the Divine with powers, wonders, and signs that the Divine did through him in your midst, as you yourselves know—
this one, handed over by the determined plan and foreknowledge of the Divine, you fastened up through lawless hands and killed.
The Divine raised him up, loosening the pains of death, because it was not possible for him to be held by it.
For David says concerning him: I saw the Lord before me through all; because he is at my right hand, so that I may not be shaken.
Because of this my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced; moreover my flesh will also dwell in hope.
Because you will not abandon my soul/life to Hades, nor give your Holy One to see decay.
You made known to me ways of life; you will fill me with gladness with your face.
Men, siblings, it is permitted to speak with openness to you concerning the patriarch David, that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is among us until this day.
Being therefore a prophet, and knowing that the Divine had sworn with an oath to him to seat one from the fruit of his loins upon his throne,
foreseeing, he spoke concerning the resurrection of the Messiah, that he was not abandoned to Hades, nor did his flesh see decay.
This Jesus the Divine raised up, of which we all are witnesses.
Therefore being exalted to the right hand of the Divine, and having received from the Father the promise of the holy Spirit, he poured out this which you both see and hear.
For David did not ascend into the heavens, but he himself says: The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand,
until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
Let all the house of Israel therefore know securely that the Divine made him both Lord and Messiah, this Jesus whom you crucified.
Hearing this, they were pierced in the heart and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, Men, siblings, what should we do?
Peter said to them, Turn/realign, and let each of you be baptized in the name of Jesus Messiah for release of your sins/misalignments, and you will receive the gift of the holy Spirit.
For the promise is to you and to your children and to all those far away, as many as the Lord our Divine may call.
With many other words he bore witness and urged them, saying, Be saved/healed from this crooked generation.
Therefore those receiving his word were baptized, and about three thousand souls/lives were added that day.
They were persevering in the teaching of the apostles and the fellowship, the breaking of bread and the prayers.
Awe came upon every soul/life, and many wonders and signs were happening through the apostles.
All those trusting were together and had all things common.
They were selling possessions and goods and distributing them to all, as anyone had need.
Day by day, persevering with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread house by house, they shared food with gladness and simplicity of heart,
praising the Divine and having favor with all the people. And the Lord was adding day by day those being saved/healed together.
